howItWorks.intro: This calculator uses heuristic analysis to estimate how likely AI detection tools (GPTZero, Originality.ai, Turnitin) would flag your text. It analyzes the same linguistic features these tools look for.
rules: AI text tends to have uniform sentence lengths — human writing varies more in rhythm and structure
AI frequently uses certain phrases: 'delve into', 'it's important to note', 'in today's world', 'landscape', 'leverage'
Low vocabulary diversity (many repeated common words) is a strong AI signal
AI text tends toward medium-length sentences (15-25 words) and avoids very short or very long ones
howItWorks.outro: No detector is perfect. AI detection tools have false positive rates of 5-15%, meaning human-written text is sometimes flagged incorrectly. This tool provides an estimate, not a guarantee. The best approach is to add your genuine voice, specific examples, and personal experience to any text.

faq.question: How accurate are AI detection tools?
faq.answer: Most AI detectors claim 85-98% accuracy, but independent testing shows real-world accuracy of 60-85%, with significant false positive rates (5-15%). They work best on unedited AI text and struggle with AI text that has been humanized or edited. No detector is reliable enough for high-stakes decisions.

faq.question: What makes text look AI-generated?
faq.answer: Key signals include: uniform sentence length, overuse of transitional phrases, certain vocabulary choices ('delve', 'landscape', 'leverage', 'crucial'), lack of personal anecdotes or specific examples, overly balanced paragraph structure, and a tendency to hedge with qualifiers. AI text also tends to lack the messy complexity of genuine human thought.

faq.question: How can I make AI text less detectable?
faq.answer: Add personal voice and specific examples from real experience. Vary sentence length dramatically — mix very short punchy sentences with longer complex ones. Use colloquial language and contractions. Remove common AI phrases. Add opinions, humor, or emotional language. Restructure paragraphs to be less formulaic.

faq.question: Does this tool actually detect AI?
faq.answer: This tool estimates detection probability using the same heuristics that AI detectors use — it does not determine with certainty whether text was AI-generated. It is a useful guide for understanding what triggers detectors, but should not be used as definitive proof of AI or human authorship.

faq.question: Is the AI Detection Score Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the AI Detection Score Calculator?
sections.body: The AI Detection Score Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your your text, content type, you get instant results including detection probability, risk level, sentence variance. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting detection probability right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your your text and need to find the right detection probability. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
